goal: Run 7 specialized audit agents independently across the codebase to identify all mobile issues, layout bugs, scroll problems, image issues, animation conflicts, and design opportunities.
summary:
Phase 1 complete. All 7 audit agents have produced their findings:
- Agent 1 (Mobile UX): 47 issues, 12 critical. All pages broken at 320px-375px.
- Agent 2 (Layout): 47 vulnerabilities, 18 critical. w-max overflows, negative transforms, fixed widths.
- Agent 3 (Scroll): No scroll-to-top on route change. FloatingMagicalArrow blocks taps.
- Agent 4 (Images): No srcset, no lazy loading, h-[118%] overflow, aspect-[21/9] too short.
- Agent 5 (Animation): blur filter CRITICAL, dead MagicParticles, 400ms polling, StrictMode double-mount.
- Agent 6 (Mobile Design): Full mobile-first spec with bottom nav, reduced typography, vintage UI.
- Agent 7 (Vintage Variants): 9 sections need mobile variants, 5 sections adaptable with responsive classes.
stepSummaries:
1. Agent 1 — Mobile UX Auditor: audit all pages at 320px–768px for horizontal scroll, overflow, text overlap, broken layouts, tiny typography, bad touch targets, broken navigation [verified]
  2. Agent 2 — Layout & Responsiveness Inspector: audit every component for fixed widths, min/max-width issues, negative margins, overflow bugs, grid/flexbox issues, breakpoint failures [pending]
  3. Agent 3 — Scroll & Navigation Specialist: audit scroll restoration, route changes, AnimatePresence, body overflow, scroll lock, trapped scrolling [pending]
  4. Agent 4 — Image & Media Specialist: audit photography pages, hero images, gallery images, card images, background images for sizing, aspect ratios, mobile scaling [pending]
  5. Agent 5 — Animation & Performance Specialist: audit Framer Motion, particles, parallax, reveal animations for layout shifts, overflow, jank, scroll interruptions [pending]
  6. Agent 6 — Mobile Design Architect: design dedicated mobile-first solutions for content hierarchy, spacing, readability, image presentation, navigation, touch interactions [pending]
  7. Agent 7 — Vintage Experience Specialist: identify sections that cannot be adapted cleanly and design mobile vintage variants [pending]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.evidence: Agent 1 report: 47 issues (12 Critical, 15 High, 12 Medium, 8 Low). Agent 2 report: 47 layout vulnerabilities (18 Critical, 8 High, 13 Medium, 8 Low). Agent 3 report: CRITICAL scroll restoration broken, no scrollTo(0,0). Agent 4 report: CRITICAL no srcset, w=2400 images on 320px screens. Agent 5 report: CRITICAL blur filter, dead MagicParticles, multiple useScroll hooks. Agent 6 report: 30KB mobile design spec with bottom nav, reduced typography, vintage aesthetic. Agent 7 report: 9 sections need mobile variants, 5 sections adaptable.
gateVerdicts.rationale: All 7 agents produced comprehensive reports stored in .kimchi/ferments/019eb201-99ea-77ed-80ea-1db0389680a3/docs/ covering all requested audit areas

gateVerdicts.evidence: Phase goal was to run 7 specialized audit agents independently across the codebase. All 7 completed their reports: agent1-mobile-ux-audit.md, agent2-layout-audit.md, agent3-scroll-audit.md, agent4-image-audit.md, agent5-animation-audit.md, agent6-mobile-design.md, agent7-vintage-variants.md
gateVerdicts.rationale: The combined output of all 7 agents provides a complete picture of every mobile issue, layout bug, scroll problem, image concern, animation conflict, and design opportunity

gateVerdicts.evidence: No items deferred. All agents completed their full scope. Phase 2 (Merge Findings) will consume all 7 reports.
gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing was left undone in Phase 1. All 7 agents ran to completion.
